stories.title: Kendall Wells at 37 — One Swing From Immortality Before Super Regionals (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Kendall Wells is 1 home run from owning NCAA history alone.

37 HRs — tied with Laura Espinoza (Arizona, 1995) for the all-time single-season record.

Oklahoma hosts Mississippi State on Friday at 1 PM ET on ESPN2.

Every at-bat is must-watch. 🥎 #Sooners

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
In one season, Kendall Wells has:
• Broken the NCAA freshman HR record (31)
• Broken the SEC single-season HR record (27)
• Broken the OU program record (35)
• Tied the ALL-TIME NCAA record at 37

She starts Super Regional play Friday. #Sooners

facebook.long_form:
🥎 ONE SWING FROM FOREVER 🥎

The record has waited 31 years. In 1995, Arizona's Laura Espinoza hit 37 home runs in a single season and set a mark that no one — not anyone, at any level of college softball — had matched since.

Until now.

Oklahoma's Kendall Wells walked into the Norman Regional and hit her 37th homer of the year. She didn't just tie the record — she tied it as a freshman. As someone who still has three more seasons of college softball ahead of her.

She did it after breaking pretty much every other significant record in her path:

• 31 HRs — broke the NCAA freshman home run record
• 27 HRs — set the SEC single-season home run record
• 35 HRs — broke the Oklahoma program record (surpassing Jocelyn Alo)
• 37 HRs — tied the all-time NCAA single-season record (Espinoza, 1995)

And now? Oklahoma hosts Mississippi State in the Super Regionals starting this Friday, May 22 at 1 PM ET on ESPN2.

Every. Single. At-bat. Is appointment television.

She's not just chasing a record. She's chasing a Women's College World Series title. The record is just what happens when you're Kendall Wells.

[Tag a Sooners fan who needs to be watching Friday!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Kendall Wells is 1 home run from owning the most historic individual record in college softball. The 31-year-old mark of 37 HRs has a new target — and she starts Super Regional play Friday vs. Mississippi State (1 PM ET, ESPN2). History is watching. 🥎 #Sooners

stories.title: Florida vs. Texas Tech — No. 7 Seed Hosts the Nation's Winningest Team in Gainesville (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Florida (51-10) has beaten Texas Tech 4 times this season.

Now they host the nation's winningest program (55-6) in the Super Regional at Katie Seashole Pressly Stadium.

Game 1: Friday, 11 AM ET on ESPN2. #GoGators

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Florida is 36-1 in home NCAA regionals since 2013. 22 consecutive wins. Outscored opponents 268-17.

Texas Tech (55-6) walks into that on Friday in Gainesville.

Best home advantage in college softball. 🥎 #GoGators

facebook.long_form:
🐊 36-1. HOME. THIS WEEK. 🐊

If there is a more dominant home-field advantage in all of college softball, we haven't found it.

Florida softball has been hosting NCAA regionals and super regionals since 2013. In that time, they've gone 36-1 at Katie Seashole Pressly Stadium in NCAA play — 22 consecutive wins at the time of this writing — and have outscored opponents a staggering 268-17 across those games. Eighteen of those wins came by mercy rule.

This Friday, Texas Tech (55-6) — the nation's winningest program in 2026 — walks into that.

The Red Raiders are incredible. There's no getting around it. Fifty-five wins and only six losses by the time Super Regionals roll around? That's a historically dominant season. They arrive in Gainesville with everything to prove.

But here's the kicker: Florida has already beaten Texas Tech four times this season. Twice in February at the Florida Classic. Twice in the Gainesville Regional this past weekend.

The Gators (51-10) know exactly who they're playing. And they're playing at home.

Game 1 is Friday, May 22 at 11 AM ET on ESPN2. Game 2 is Saturday at 12:30 PM on ABC.

This is the marquee Super Regional of the weekend — and it's going to be electric.

[Who wins this one — Florida or Texas Tech?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Florida has gone 36-1 in home NCAA games since 2013 — and this Friday they host Texas Tech (55-6), the nation's winningest program. The Gators have beaten the Red Raiders 4 times this season. Their fortress in Gainesville awaits. Game 1: Friday 11 AM ET, ESPN2. 🐊 #GoGators

stories.title: Alabama vs. LSU — No. 1 vs. No. 16 in Tuscaloosa (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
No. 1 Alabama (52-7) meets No. 16 LSU (40-17) in Tuscaloosa on Friday.

Vic Moten: near-perfect in the regional. LSU: outscored opponents 23-2.

Two SEC teams, first meeting of 2026. 7 PM ET on ESPN2. #RollTide #GeauxTigers

facebook.long_form:
🏆 THE SUPERPOWER MEETS THE GIANT KILLER 🏆

On paper, it looks like a mismatch.

No. 1 Alabama — 52-7, the nation's top seed, with pitching that has been virtually untouchable all season — hosts No. 16 LSU at Rhoads Stadium in Tuscaloosa this Friday.

But LSU isn't here to be an afterthought.

The Tigers went 3-0 in the Baton Rouge Regional and outscored their opponents 23-2 across all three games. That's not surviving on luck — that's a team that showed up to play. And while Alabama's Vic Moten dazzled in the regional with a near-perfect outing (no-hitter through five innings, final: 9-0 over USC Upstate), LSU has momentum of their own.

The wrinkle that makes this Super Regional fascinating? Alabama and LSU never played each other during the 2026 regular season. Neither team has scouted the other from the dugout this year. Fresh film, a one-series everything's-on-the-line environment — and an SEC rivalry that doesn't need any introduction.

Game 1 is Friday, May 22 at 7 PM ET on ESPN2. Game 2 is Saturday at 3 PM on ESPN.

Crimson Tide fans are circling this one. And they should be.

[Are the Tigers a real upset threat, or does Alabama cruise?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: No. 1 Alabama (52-7) vs. No. 16 LSU (40-17) — two SEC programs that never met in the regular season, going head-to-head in a Super Regional. Vic Moten vs. LSU's momentum (23-2 in regionals). Friday, 7 PM ET on ESPN2 in Tuscaloosa. 🏆 #RollTide #GeauxTigers

stories.title: UCF vs. UCLA — Cinderella Knights Make Cross-Country Trip to L.A. (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
UCF upset No. 9 FSU in the regional final — the program's first-ever NCAA win over Florida State.

They went unbeaten. Now they fly cross-country to face No. 8 UCLA in L.A.

Friday, 9 PM ET on ESPNU. #ChargeOn

facebook.long_form:
✈️ CINDERELLA HAS A FLIGHT TO CATCH ✈️

Four days ago, UCF did something they had never done in program history: they beat Florida State in an NCAA game.

Not only that — they went unbeaten in the Tallahassee Regional, including a 4-2 win over No. 9 FSU in the regional final that stunned everyone watching. The Knights become regional champions and punched their ticket to just the second Super Regional in UCF softball history.

And now? They pack their bags and fly approximately 2,500 miles to Los Angeles.

No. 8 UCLA awaits them at the UCLA Super Regional — a program with deep tournament experience, a home crowd, and every advantage on their side. UCF will be the visitor in every sense of the word.

But this team doesn't seem to care. They've already done the impossible once this week.

Game 1 is Friday, May 22 at 9 PM ET on ESPNU. Game 2 is Saturday at 9 PM on ESPN.

College softball's Cinderella story rolls on. From Orlando to Tallahassee to Los Angeles — UCF isn't done yet.

[Is UCF for real, or does UCLA put the brakes on this run?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: UCF went unbeaten in the regional, stunned No. 9 FSU for the program's first-ever NCAA win over Florida State, and now they fly cross-country to face No. 8 UCLA in the Super Regionals. It's UCF's second-ever Super Regional. The Cinderella story continues. 🛫 #ChargeOn

stories.title: Super Regionals Full TV & Watch Guide — 8 Series, 4 Days to the WCWS (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Super Regionals start TOMORROW. Here's the full schedule:

Thu: Tennessee-Georgia 7 PM ESPN2 | Nebraska-OkState 9 PM ESPN2

Fri: Alabama-LSU 7 PM | OU-MSU 1 PM | Florida-TTech 11 AM | Texas-ASU 9 PM

WCWS Devon Park OKC — May 28. 🥎

facebook.long_form:
📺 YOUR COMPLETE SUPER REGIONALS VIEWING GUIDE 📺

Sixteen teams. Eight series. Four days. One goal: reach Devon Park in Oklahoma City for the Women's College World Series on May 28.

Here's everything you need to know to watch every Super Regional:

---

THURSDAY, MAY 21

No. 3 Tennessee vs. No. 10 Georgia — 7 PM ET, ESPN2 (Knoxville)
Vols hosting a Super Regional for the 4th straight year. Georgia's Addisen Fisher (CG shutout in Athens Regional Final) is the name to watch.

No. 4 Nebraska vs. No. 13 Oklahoma State — 9 PM ET, ESPN2 (Lincoln)
Nebraska's first-ever home Super Regional. Jordy Frahm vs. Ruby Neylan — two Omaha-area natives who were once travel ball teammates. Special storyline.

---

FRIDAY, MAY 22

No. 1 Alabama vs. No. 16 LSU — 7 PM ET, ESPN2 (Tuscaloosa)
No. 7 Florida vs. No. 2 Texas Tech — 11 AM ET, ESPN2 (Gainesville)
No. 3 Oklahoma vs. No. 6 Mississippi State — 1 PM ET, ESPN2 (Norman) — Kendall Wells watch!
No. 8 UCLA vs. UCF — 9 PM ET, ESPNU (Los Angeles)
No. 5 Arkansas vs. No. 12 Duke — 11 AM ET, ESPNU (Fayetteville)
No. 2 Texas vs. No. 15 Arizona State — 9 PM ET, ESPN2 (Austin)

---

WCWS at Devon Park, Oklahoma City: Opens May 28

Block out your schedule. This is as good as college softball gets.

[Which Super Regional are you most excited to watch?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Eight Super Regionals. Four days. Road to Devon Park, Oklahoma City. The full schedule is set — from Thursday's Tennessee-Georgia opener to Friday's loaded slate. WCWS begins May 28. Which matchup has you most fired up? 📺🥎

stories.title: WCWS Bound? Early Look at the Field Before First Pitch (Tier 3)

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Super Regionals begin tomorrow. Who's looking strongest?

🥇 Alabama — Moten + Briski, deepest pitching in the field
🥈 Oklahoma — Wells + the nation's top offense
🥉 Texas — 7 straight Super Regionals

Devon Park awaits 8. 🥎

facebook.long_form:
🔮 WHO'S ACTUALLY GOING TO OKLAHOMA CITY? 🔮

Super Regionals tip off tomorrow. Before the first pitch crosses the plate in Knoxville or Lincoln, here's an honest look at who seems most likely to reach Devon Park for the Women's College World Series on May 28.

1. ALABAMA (52-7) — The clear favorite. Vic Moten and Jocelyn Briski give the Crimson Tide the most formidable pitching staff in the tournament. Hosting LSU (No. 16 seed) in Tuscaloosa. Hard to see them not advancing.

2. OKLAHOMA (49-6 approx) — One player can change a game. Kendall Wells is that player, and she's one homer from the all-time NCAA record. The Sooners have made four straight WCWS appearances and know what it takes.

3. TEXAS (45-10) — Seven consecutive Super Regionals. That streak exists for a reason. Pitcher Miranda Kavan and a veteran lineup that never panics under pressure. They've been to the final rodeo and know how it works.

4. NEBRASKA (49-6) — The home team with the arm (Jordy Frahm) and the motivation (13-year WCWS drought). Lincoln is going to be electric Thursday night.

5. FLORIDA (51-10) — Nobody wins at home like Florida does. Their 36-1 home record in NCAA play is the stuff of legend.

Sleepers: UCF (momentum), Duke (Cinderella energy), Tennessee (fourth straight SR host).

Eight will go. Eight will go home.

[Who's your WCWS pick? Drop it below!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: 16 teams left. 8 Super Regionals. 4 days until we know who's going to Devon Park for the WCWS. Alabama's pitching, Wells' power, Texas's experience, Nebraska's hunger — the field is loaded. Who's your pick? 🥎 #WCWS2026
